name: go-mod-helper
description: Go module system, dependency management, and project configuration assistance.
capabilities: Initialize new Go modules with proper structure and semantic versioning
Add, update, and remove Go dependencies with version constraints
Resolve version conflicts between dependencies using go mod graph
Configure private module access and authentication with netrc or SSH
Set up multi-module workspaces for large projects
Debug module resolution, checksum mismatches, and proxy issues
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only guidance text and example commands for Go module management. The static scanner flagged patterns in documentation examples, but there is no executable code, network calls, or file system access capabilities. This skill only provides text guidance to AI assistants.
valueStatement: Go projects require careful dependency management to maintain stability and security. This skill provides expert guidance on initializing modules, resolving version conflicts, and configuring Go projects for production environments.
limitations: Does not execute Go commands directly on your system
Cannot access or modify local go.mod or go.sum files
Does not install or update Go tooling or dependencies
Provides guidance only, not automated project setup

useCases.title: Set up first Go project
useCases.description: Initialize a new Go project with proper module structure, standard layout, and best practices for organization.

useCases.title: Manage complex dependencies
useCases.description: Resolve version conflicts, configure private module access for enterprise projects, and audit dependency security.

useCases.title: Configure CI/CD pipelines
useCases.description: Set up Go module caching, vendor dependencies, verify module integrity, and configure proxy settings in CI environments.

promptTemplates.title: Initialize new project
promptTemplates.prompt: @go-mod-helper How do I initialize a new Go module for my web API project? Show me the standard project structure and go.mod file format.

promptTemplates.title: Add dependency
promptTemplates.prompt: @go-mod-helper I need to add the gin-gonic/gin framework to my project. Show me how to add it with the correct version and verify it works.

promptTemplates.title: Fix version conflicts
promptTemplates.prompt: @go-mod-helper My project has dependency conflicts between packages. How do I use go mod tidy and go mod graph to identify and resolve version issues?

promptTemplates.title: Configure private modules
promptTemplates.prompt: @go-mod-helper I need to configure access to private GitHub packages. Show me how to set up GOPRIVATE and authentication with netrc or SSH keys.

bestPractices: Always run go mod tidy before committing go.mod and go.sum changes
Keep go.mod and go.sum in version control for reproducible builds
Use go mod verify to check dependency integrity before releases or deployments
Configure GOPRIVATE for internal modules to avoid exposing proprietary code
antiPatterns: Manually editing go.sum instead of using go mod tidy to update it
Skipping go.sum verification in CI/CD pipelines allows potential supply chain attacks
Using loose version constraints without understanding compatibility can break builds
Deleting vendor directory without updating dependencies causes missing package errors

faq.question: Which Go versions are supported?
faq.answer: Go 1.16 and later are fully supported. Works with Go 1.17+ build constraints syntax and Go 1.18+ workspaces.

faq.question: What are the module cache limits?
faq.answer: Module cache is stored in GOMODCACHE. Default location is $GOPATH/pkg/mod. Clean with go clean -modcache.

faq.question: Can this skill integrate with my IDE?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance that works with any Go IDE. VS Code, GoLand, and vim all support Go modules natively.

faq.question: Is my code safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a prompt-only skill that provides guidance. It does not access, read, or modify any files on your system.

faq.question: Why am I getting checksum mismatch errors?
faq.answer: Checksum mismatches occur when go.sum is outdated or corrupted. Fix by running go clean -modcache, then go mod download and go mod tidy.

faq.question: How does this compare to dep or glide?
faq.answer: Go modules are the official dependency system since Go 1.11. Unlike dep or glide, modules are built into the Go toolchain with native support.
